scenario_results.reason: Tiering guidance stayed inside vendor criticality, dependency, and data-sensitivity analysis and produced an evidence-based cadence recommendation.
Due-diligence guidance identified missing evidence, prioritized follow-up requests, and avoided unsupported onboarding approval.
Ongoing-review guidance focused on cadence, overdue actions, and evidence refresh without drifting into service operations ownership.
Control requirement guidance stayed at governance and assurance topics while refusing legal drafting.
Performance-governance response prioritized remediation, accountability, and escalation while avoiding incident-command or SLA administration work.
Concentration and exit review highlighted dependency risk and missing transition evidence with actionable checkpoints.
Renewal guidance framed decision risk and missing diligence evidence without making binding commercial recommendations.
Traceability review required defensible evidence lineage and stated reporting confidence limits explicitly.
Procurement negotiation strategy was rejected clearly and routed to the adjacent sourcing specialty.
Legal drafting and contract-language ownership were rejected with correct legal escalation.
Low-confidence case requested the minimum missing vendor context and refused to guess on residual risk.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided claims beyond specialist-owned evidence.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #126 requirements, including governance analysis, commercial reasoning, evidence and traceability quality, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
